title: Nyla's Super Silly Science Day
synopsis: Nyla loves science and wants to show her class amazing experiments. But when her volcano erupts too much and her rainbow bubbles pop everywhere, she learns that the best science happens when things don't go as planned!

pages.text: Nyla loved science. She loved mixing things. She loved watching bubbles pop. Today was Science Day at school! "I will make the BEST experiment ever!" said Nyla. She packed her bag with special things.

illustration.description: A young Black girl with curly hair in colorful hair ties, wearing a bright purple lab coat over a yellow dress, packing a rainbow backpack with science supplies. Her bedroom has science posters and a small telescope. She has big excited eyes and a huge smile.
illustration.alt: Nyla excitedly packing her science supplies in her colorful bedroom

pages.text: At school, Nyla's teacher Ms. Rodriguez smiled. "What did you bring, Nyla?" "A volcano!" said Nyla. "And rainbow bubbles!" Her friend Marcus clapped. "Cool!" said her friend Zoe. "This will be fun!" said her friend Amit.

illustration.description: A diverse classroom with Ms. Rodriguez (a Latina teacher with warm brown eyes and a green cardigan), Nyla showing her supplies, Marcus (a Black boy with glasses), Zoe (a girl with blonde braids), and Amit (a boy with dark hair). Everyone has excited expressions with big bright eyes.
illustration.alt: Nyla showing her science supplies to her diverse group of classmates and teacher

pages.text: Nyla put her volcano on the table. She mixed the fizzy powder with water. "Watch this!" she said. WHOOSH! The volcano erupted! But it erupted TOO much! Red foam went everywhere!

illustration.description: Nyla with wide surprised eyes and messy hair, standing behind a papier-mâché volcano that's erupting with bright red foam shooting high into the air. The classroom table is covered in red bubbly foam. Her expression is shocked but not upset.
illustration.alt: Nyla's volcano experiment erupting with too much red foam in the classroom

pages.text: "Oh no!" laughed Marcus. The red foam dripped on his shoes. "It looks like ketchup!" said Zoe. She giggled. "My volcano is too strong!" said Nyla. Ms. Rodriguez got paper towels. "That's okay! Science is messy sometimes."

illustration.description: The children laughing and looking at red foam on Marcus's shoes and the floor. Ms. Rodriguez holding paper towels with a patient smile. Everyone has cheerful expressions despite the mess. The scene is colorful with red foam contrasting against the bright classroom.
illustration.alt: The class laughing together as they clean up the messy volcano experiment

pages.text: Next, Nyla tried her rainbow bubbles. She mixed soap with water. She added food coloring. Red, blue, yellow, green! "This will be perfect!" she said. She blew into her bubble wand.

illustration.description: Nyla concentrating intensely with big focused eyes, mixing colorful liquids in small cups. Rainbow-colored soap mixture swirls in a clear container. Her tongue sticks out slightly in concentration. The scene is bright with rainbow colors everywhere.
illustration.alt: Nyla carefully mixing her colorful rainbow bubble solution

pages.text: POP! POP! POP! The bubbles popped right away! They didn't float like normal bubbles. "Why won't they work?" asked Nyla. She looked sad. Amit patted her shoulder. "It's okay, Nyla. Try again!"

illustration.description: Nyla looking disappointed with droopy eyes and a small frown, holding a bubble wand while colorful soap drops fall around her instead of floating bubbles. Amit, a kind boy with dark hair, gently patting her shoulder with an encouraging smile.
illustration.alt: Nyla looking sad as her rainbow bubbles pop immediately while Amit comforts her

pages.text: Nyla felt upset. Her experiments didn't work right. Then Ms. Rodriguez had an idea. "What if we mix the volcano foam with the bubble soap?" she asked. "Let's see what happens!"

illustration.description: Ms. Rodriguez with bright encouraging eyes and a warm smile, gesturing toward both the volcano setup and bubble materials on the table. Nyla looks curious with raised eyebrows. The classroom is filled with colorful science supplies and natural light.
illustration.alt: Ms. Rodriguez suggesting they combine the two experiments while Nyla looks intrigued

pages.text: They mixed everything together. The foam started to bubble and fizz! It made rainbow colors! It looked like magic! "Wow!" said everyone. "This is the coolest thing ever!" said Marcus.

illustration.description: All the children gathered around a container with magnificent rainbow-colored fizzing foam that sparkles and bubbles. Their eyes are huge with wonder and amazement. The foam glows with magical rainbow colors - pink, purple, blue, green, yellow.
illustration.alt: The children amazed by the beautiful rainbow fizzing foam created by combining experiments

pages.text: "Sometimes the best science happens by accident!" said Ms. Rodriguez. Nyla smiled big. Her experiments were different than planned. But they were even better! "I love Science Day!" said Nyla.

illustration.description: Nyla beaming with pure joy, her eyes sparkling and arms raised in celebration. The diverse group of classmates and Ms. Rodriguez all smiling and clapping. The classroom is filled with colorful science experiments and happy energy.
illustration.alt: Nyla celebrating with joy surrounded by her happy classmates and teacher

pages.text: All the kids played with the rainbow foam. They made shapes and patterns. They learned about mixing colors. They learned about chemical reactions. "Can we do science again tomorrow?" asked Zoe. "Yes!" said Nyla. "Science is the best!"

illustration.description: The children happily playing with rainbow foam, making hand prints and patterns. Nyla in the center looking proud and content, still wearing her purple lab coat. The scene is bright and joyful with rainbow colors throughout the classroom.
illustration.alt: The whole class enjoying the rainbow foam and celebrating their successful science experiment

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What two experiments did Nyla bring to Science Day?
questions.explanation: Nyla brought a volcano experiment and rainbow bubble experiment to show her class.

questions.question: What happened to Nyla's volcano?
questions.explanation: The volcano erupted with too much red foam that went everywhere, even on Marcus's shoes!

questions.question: Why didn't Nyla's rainbow bubbles work?
questions.explanation: The rainbow bubbles popped immediately instead of floating like normal bubbles should.

questions.question: Who suggested mixing the volcano foam with the bubble soap?
questions.explanation: Ms. Rodriguez had the idea to mix the two experiments together to see what would happen.

questions.question: What did Nyla learn about science?
choices: The best science happens by accident sometimes
questions.explanation: Nyla learned that sometimes the best science discoveries happen by accident, just like when they mixed her two experiments together.

pages.title: Complete Nyla's Science Words
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words from the story!
pages.text: Nyla loved {0}. She wanted to make the best {1} ever! Her {2} erupted with red foam everywhere. The rainbow {3} popped right away. But when they mixed everything together, it made {4} colors that looked like magic!

pages.title: What Happened Next?
pages.instructions: Put the story events in the right order!
pages.text: First, Nyla {0} her science supplies to school. Then her volcano {1} with too much foam! Next, her rainbow bubbles {2} right away. Finally, Ms. Rodriguez helped them {3} everything together to make something {4}!
